_comment: The founder-story funnel. type: VALUE = pure usefulness, no app. INTRO = personal/founder/build-in-public. SOFT_APP = honest, disclosed app mention. COMMUNITY = question to spark replies. Weeks 1-2 establish trust; weeks 3-4 run the ongoing 70/20/10 mix. Add weekN keys freely — the engine reads them all.

week1.topic: Hello — who I am and why I'm here
week1.brief: Short founder intro. I work in disability support, I'm here to share useful stuff for sole traders. No pitch. Invite people to say hi.

week1.topic: The 3 admin tasks that quietly eat your week
week1.brief: Practical breakdown + one tip for each. Pure value.

week1.topic: What's the most confusing part of the NDIS price guide for you?
week1.brief: Open question. Reply to every comment personally.

week1.topic: A clean progress-note structure that survives an audit
week1.brief: Give away an actual template/structure. No strings. Never say 'audit-proof' or 'guarantees compliance'.

week2.topic: Getting paid faster: invoicing + claims checklist
The admin night that made me start building something
week2.brief: Honest founder story — the pain that started it. Still no hard pitch.

week2.topic: Solo support work can be isolating — how do you stay connected?
What I've been building — want to be first to try it?
week2.brief: Disclosed, PRE-LAUNCH invite. 'I built this, here's what it does, it's not out yet — would love a few providers to shape it and be first in line.' NO live product link yet; invite to register interest / DM. Honest it's still being readied for market.

week3.topic: Travel and the bits people forget to claim
week3.brief: Provider travel, non-labour km, short-notice cancellations — always per the CURRENT pricing arrangements. Keep a travel log as you go. No guarantees, point to official guide.

week3.topic: Capturing notes during a shift, not at 9pm
week3.brief: Capture-now-polish-later. Generic tools (voice memo, notes app, paper). Three bullets beat a blank page. No app pitch.

week3.topic: What eats your week that has nothing to do with actual support?
week3.brief: Open question on time-wasters. Reply personally.

week3.topic: Build-in-public: the feedback that changed what I'm building
week3.brief: Honest build-in-public. A worker's comment reshaped a Caira decision. Pre-launch, still shaping it. End with a question.

week4.brief: Quick respectful-language guide; ask the person how they describe themselves. Offer the one-page cheat-sheet lead magnet. Pure value.
Calm, factual: what/when/who/what you did/who you told. Observable facts only. Know your provider's reporting timeframes. No guarantees.

week4.topic: Best advice you got when you started as a support worker?
week4.brief: Community wisdom for new workers. Reply personally.

week4.topic: Caira update — early feedback is making it sharper
week4.brief: Disclosed pre-launch update. Early providers giving feedback; tagline sign-off. Taking a few more to shape it. Comment/DM. No cost.

week5.topic: Setting your rates as a sole trader
week5.brief: How to think about rates within the current NDIS pricing arrangements; know the caps, factor real costs (travel, admin, super). General guidance, not financial advice. No guarantees.

week5.topic: Professional boundaries with participants and families
week5.brief: Warm but clear boundaries; off-hours messages and gifts; protects you and the relationship. Pure value.

week5.topic: How do you reset after a shift that went sideways?
Build-in-public: first beta workers are in
week5.brief: Honest milestone — first support workers using Caira. What surprised me. Still pre-launch. End with a question.

week6.topic: Tax and GST basics for sole-trader support workers
week6.brief: Plain-English overview: ABN, the $75k GST threshold, keeping receipts, setting aside for tax. NOT financial advice — see a registered accountant. No guarantees.
Practical: confirmations, clear cancellation terms in your agreement, gentle reminders. Know the current short-notice cancellation rules. Pure value.

week6.topic: What got you into support work?
Caira pre-launch: a few early spots left
week6.brief: Disclosed pre-launch invite/update. Shaping it with early users; a few spots to be first in line. Comment/DM. No cost. Tagline sign-off.

week7.topic: Handover notes when more than one worker is on
week7.brief: What a useful handover/continuity note includes so the next worker isn't guessing. Factual, participant-centred. Pure value.

week7.topic: Spotting burnout before it spots you
week7.brief: Signs of burnout in support work; small protective habits; it's okay to ask for help. Warm, no hype.

week7.topic: Share a small win from this week
Build-in-public: what building solo has taught me
week7.brief: Honest reflection on building Caira solo while working. Pre-launch. End with a question.

week8.topic: Goal-focused notes: linking what you do to a participant's goals
week8.brief: How to tie the support you give to the participant's NDIS goals in your notes — clearer, more meaningful. Factual. No guarantees.

week8.topic: Keeping your checks and screening current
week8.brief: Practical reminder list: NDIS Worker Screening Check, working-with-children where relevant, first aid, renewals. Know your obligations; check official sources. No guarantees.

week8.topic: If your admin could vanish overnight, what goes first?
week8.brief: Playful but pointed question on admin pain. Reply personally.

week8.topic: Caira — what the first users are saying
week8.brief: Disclosed pre-launch progress post; early-user themes (no fabricated quotes — keep general until real testimonials exist). Invite to be first in line. Tagline sign-off.

week9.topic: Dignity and privacy in your notes — what NOT to include
week9.brief: Record what's relevant to support and goals; leave out gossip, unrelated medical detail, judgements. Notes can be read by others. Factual, respectful. No guarantees.

week9.topic: Juggling a schedule across several participants
week9.brief: Practical: buffer time for travel/notes, colour-coding, not double-booking, protecting admin time. Pure value.

week9.topic: How do you keep work and life separate as a sole trader?
week9.brief: Conversation on switching off. Reply personally.
Honest — a feature I built then cut because users didn't want it. Lesson in listening. Pre-launch. End with a question.

week10.topic: Working well with support coordinators and plan managers
week10.brief: How to communicate clearly, what they need from you, timely useful updates. Builds the buyer relationship too. Pure value.

week10.topic: Duty of care and emergencies on shift
week10.brief: Stay calm, ensure safety first, follow your training and provider's procedures, document and report. Know your plan before you need it. No guarantees; not a substitute for proper training.

week10.topic: What's a myth about support work you wish people understood?

week10.brief: Disclosed update: closer to launch, early group shaping it, a few spots to be first in line. Comment/DM. No cost. Tagline sign-off.

week11.topic: Record-keeping for tax time — what to keep and for how long
week11.brief: Keep invoices, receipts, logbooks; general retention guidance; a simple system beats shoeboxes. NOT financial/legal advice — check with an accountant/ATO. No guarantees.

week11.topic: Building rapport with a new participant in week one
week11.brief: Go slow, listen, follow their lead, small consistency builds trust. Warm, practical. Pure value.

week11.topic: Best book, podcast or group for support workers?
Build-in-public: nearly there — what's left before launch
week11.brief: Honest near-launch update: what's done, what's left, gratitude to early users. Pre-launch. End with a question.

week12.topic: Understanding a participant's plan — the basics
week12.brief: Plan-managed vs self vs agency, funding categories at a high level, why it matters for what you can do. General info, check official sources. No guarantees.
Respect, ask don't assume, learn the person's preferences, work with not over them. Warm, practical. Pure value.

week12.topic: Three months in — thank you, and what do you want more of?
week12.brief: Gratitude + ask the community what content they want next. Reply personally.
Disclosed update; honest status (adjust to whether launched). Invite to join/be first in line. Tagline sign-off. No guarantees, no fabricated claims.
